 {"id":"bd-274o","title":"demo_showcase page: Settings (theme, toggles, about)","description":"A real Settings page that proves the app is configurable, themeable, and polished.

Why:
- Live theme switching is one of the most compelling proofs that lipgloss theming is *real*.
- Toggles (mouse, animations, color mode) demonstrate the stack behaves well across environments (CI/headless, low-color terminals, accessibility).
- A built-in keybindings reference makes the demo usable immediately.

Must showcase:
- Theme picker:
  - preview + apply built-in presets
  - immediate app-wide update (cards, banners, chips, tables, markdown)
- Toggles that apply instantly (no restart):
  - mouse enabled/disabled
  - animations / reduce motion
  - no-color / ASCII mode
  - syntax highlighting (if feature enabled)
  - alt-screen vs no-alt-screen (or at least document this as startup-only if required)
- Keybinding reference:
  - shows global and page-specific shortcuts
  - optionally exposes a customization hook (even if not persisted yet)
- Diagnostics/about:
  - show versions, renderer/color profile, feature flags
  - credits/licenses (brief, tasteful)

Acceptance:
- Every setting has an immediate, visible effect.
- Settings UX is clean: no confusing toggles, and feedback is clear (toast/status message).

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - applying a setting updates the Config/state and triggers expected messages
  - theme switching updates a representative set of themed styles
- E2E:
  - settings toggles + theme switching scenario validates:
    - theme change affects output
    - no-color mode produces expected ASCII output
    - animations toggle changes behavior deterministically

Notes:
- Keep the page small but high-impact: fewer toggles, but each one clearly demonstrates robustness.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:23.563760533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:42:27.102146644Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","page","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-274o","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:23.577661206Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-278q","title":"demo_showcase: realistic demo data + background simulation","description":"Provide believable, dynamic data so the UI feels like a real application.

Deliverables:
- In-memory "backend" that produces:
  - services/projects, environments, deployments, jobs, logs, alerts, docs.
- Deterministic-ish randomness (seedable) so demos are stable but lively.
- Background updates (tick/every + async tasks) that change state over time.

Acceptance:
- UI can show live updates without external network dependencies.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.786578236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802547440Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-278q","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802497297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28jp","title":"demo_showcase UX: keybindings + interaction model","description":"Define an opinionated interaction model that feels premium:

- Global keys (examples):
  - `?` help overlay, `q` quit, `esc` back/close, `/` search/command palette, `t` theme switcher, `tab` focus next pane.
- Per-widget conventions: list/table navigation, viewport scrolling, form submit/cancel.
- Mouse expectations: click to focus/select; wheel scroll in viewports/lists.

-{"id":"bd-28l3","title":"Wizard: implement huh form UI embedded in app","description":"Implement the Wizard page using huh components, embedded inside the demo_showcase app chrome.

Why:
- This is the "do the thing" workflow: it makes the demo feel like a real deployment/onboarding tool.
- It showcases huh’s strengths: multi-step forms, validation, confirm/select flows.

Requirements:
- Multi-step flow as designed in `Wizard: design the multi-step workflow`:
  - a mix of input types (text, select, confirm)
  - validations with clear inline errors
- Navigation:
  - cancel/back behavior matches global UX rules (`esc` closes / backs out safely)
  - submit produces a clear success/failure state
- Presentation:
  - keyboard-first
  - mouse optional
  - visually cohesive with the app theme

Integration:
- On completion, the wizard triggers the shared domain action API (separate task) to create a deployment/job and emit logs/toasts.

Acceptance:
- Wizard feels like a real onboarding/deploy tool, not a demo form.
- Validation and recovery flows are polished (errors feel intentional).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.916099726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:11:02.104320957Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ynf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:41.872484594Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:44.500650602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.934899534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:47.373969167Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-299e","title":"E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture","description":"Add an E2E logging + artifacts foundation so failures are instantly diagnosable.

Why:
- TUIs are notoriously hard to debug from CI logs alone.
- We want every failure to answer: "what inputs happened", "what screen did we see", "what did we assert".

Deliverables:
- A `TestLogger`/`ScenarioRecorder` that records *structured* events:
  - scenario name + run id
  - step index + human-readable step description
  - injected input (key/mouse/resize) including raw bytes when relevant
  - assertions performed + expected vs actual summaries
  - captured output snapshots (last N frames + final frame)
  - active Config snapshot (seed/theme/toggles, artifacts dir)
- Output formats:
  - `events.jsonl` (machine readable) with stable keys for tooling
  - `summary.txt` (human readable) that points to the exact failing step
- Artifact directory structure (deterministic and CI-friendly):
  - `target/demo_showcase_e2e/<scenario>/<run_id>/...`
  - always write artifacts on failure; optionally keep them on success behind an env flag

Logging schema guidance (keep it simple and grep/jq-friendly):
- Required fields: `ts`, `level`, `scenario`, `run_id`, `step`, `event`, `message`
- Optional fields: `input`, `assertion`, `expected`, `actual`, `frame_path`, `config`

Acceptance:
- A failing E2E test leaves a clear trail: "what happened" + "what we expected" + the last visible screen.
- The artifact bundle is enough to reproduce locally (seed + scenario name + config snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.556281120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:01:53.301838829Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:01:53.301768869Z","close_reason":"Implemented ScenarioRecorder with structured event logging, JSONL output, artifact capture, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-299e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.586010319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b7h","title":"Unit tests: simulation determinism (no real sleeps)","description":"Add unit tests proving the demo_showcase background simulation is deterministic and test-friendly.

Why:
- The demo depends on a "fake backend" simulation. If it’s nondeterministic, tests become flaky.
- E2E scenarios also rely on deterministic evolution (tick-driven, no real sleeps).

Coverage:
- Given a fixed seed and a fixed tick delta:
  - advancing N ticks produces expected state changes (jobs progress, metrics trends, log entries)
- No wall-clock sleeps:
  - the simulation can be stepped manually (pure tick function)
- Stability:
  - no NaNs/overflow/panics with extreme tick counts or dt values

Acceptance:
- The simulation is deterministic and easy to reason about.
- Tests run fast and do not require a TTY.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:06.578799968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:15.528802419Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:15.528736406Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b7h","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:06.591863269Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b7h","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:20.337957210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3d1w","title":"Dashboard: mouse affordances (clickable cards)","description":"Add mouse affordances to the Dashboard so it feels like a modern interactive console.

Why:
- Mouse support is an "advanced" feature that many terminal apps skip.
- When done well (subtle highlights + click actions), it makes the demo feel shockingly polished.

Behaviors:
- Click to select/focus a dashboard card/widget.
- Click on obvious affordances (e.g., "View logs" / "Open job") to navigate.
- Hover/selection highlight (keyboard focus and mouse selection should be visually consistent).

Constraints:
- Must degrade gracefully when mouse is disabled (Config toggle) or terminal doesn’t support it.
- Wheel scroll should not break layout; only scroll the active viewport/list when applicable.

Acceptance:
- Click selects a card and visibly focuses it.
- Mouse interactions never steal focus unexpectedly or cause layout corruption.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.759013697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:44.024189348Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:25.679670153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.778296349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:24.421135419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

\`\`\`rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
\`\`\`

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, warn, instrument};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        command_type = ?cmd_type,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
\`\`\`

### Structured Logging Fields
\`\`\`rust
struct CommandContext {
    command_id: u64,
    command_type: &'static str,
    batch_id: Option<u64>,
}

// Log with context
debug!(
    target: "bubbletea",
    command_id = ctx.command_id,
    command_type = ctx.command_type,
    batch_id = ?ctx.batch_id,
    "Command result dropped"
);
\`\`\`

### Log Levels
- **trace**: All channel sends (very verbose, for deep debugging)
- **debug**: Failed sends during normal operation
- **warn**: Failed sends that indicate unexpected state

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing feature flag (already present)
2. Create helper function for logged send
3. Replace all \`let _ = tx.send()\` with logged version
4. Add command context tracking where feasible
5. Add documentation example
6. Add integration test for log capture

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
use tracing_test::traced_test;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn channel_disconnection_is_logged() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result dropped"));
    assert!(logs_contain("test_command"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn successful_send_logs_at_trace_level() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Trace level logs successful sends
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result sent"));
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Test
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn logs_include_command_context() {
    use tracing_subscriber::layer::SubscriberExt;
    
    let (captured, guard) = setup_log_capture();
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel);
    let cmd = Command::new(|| {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
        Some(Message::new("delayed"))
    });
    
    program.send(cmd);
    program.quit();  // Quit before command completes
    
    // Command result should be logged as dropped
    let logs = captured.into_inner();
    assert!(logs.iter().any(|l| l.contains("dropped")));
}
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: bubbletea_logging_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== bubbletea Channel Logging Tests ==="

# Enable trace logging
export RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug

# Run tests with log capture
cargo test --package bubbletea channel_disconnection -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log

# Verify expected log messages appear
if grep -q "Command result dropped" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Channel disconnection logging works"
else
    echo "[FAIL] Channel disconnection not logged"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify structured fields present
if grep -q "command_type" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Structured logging fields present"
else
    echo "[WARN] Missing structured fields - check log format"
fi

echo "=== Test Complete ==="
\`\`\`

## Documentation Example
\`\`\`rust
//! # Debugging bubbletea Command Execution
//!
//! Enable debug logging to see command execution flow:
//!
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! For even more detail:
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=trace cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! ## Log Message Reference
//!
//! | Level | Message | Meaning |
//! |-------|---------|---------|
//! | trace | "Command result sent" | Normal operation |
//! | debug | "Command result dropped" | Receiver disconnected |
//! | warn | "Command failed during active loop" | Unexpected |
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All channel send failures logged at debug level
- [ ] Logs include command context (type, id)
- [ ] Structured logging with tracing
- [ ] No performance impact when logging disabled
- [ ] Unit tests with tracing_test
- [ ] Integration test for log capture
- [ ] E2E test script validates logging
- [ ] Documentation example added","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.262130512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:33:29.920025821Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","error-handling","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d69","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.289804229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d87","title":"Docs: syntax highlighting + line numbers toggle","description":"Add syntax highlighting and optional line numbers to rendered Markdown in the Docs page.

Why:
- Syntax highlighting is a flagship Glamour feature and a major "wow" factor.
- It also demonstrates that the demo respects runtime toggles and terminal capabilities.

Requirements:
- Highlight fenced code blocks when the feature is enabled.
- Optional line numbers (toggle-able):
  - consistent width/padding
  - do not break wrapping
- Must respect:
  - no-color / ASCII mode (disable highlighting cleanly)
  - Config/Settings toggles (syntax on/off, line numbers on/off)

Performance:
- Avoid re-highlighting on every keystroke; only re-render on relevant changes (doc switch, resize, theme/toggles).

Acceptance:
- Code blocks look great and readable.
- Toggling syntax/line numbers is immediate and stable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:45.651163291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:29:17.626856556Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","glamour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d87","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:45.667314519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d87","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:36.620689828Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3ecr","title":"E2E scenario: settings toggles + theme switching","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that validates Settings actually changes the running app.

Scenario outline (headless/custom-IO):
1) Launch demo_showcase in a deterministic mode (fixed seed, animations off).
2) Navigate to Settings.
3) Toggle at least:
   - no-color / ASCII mode (or equivalent)
   - mouse enable (even if headless, ensure toggle state updates)
4) Switch theme preset and confirm global restyling takes effect.
5) Return to Dashboard (or another page) and confirm the changes persist across navigation.

Assertions:
- Output changes in expected ways (e.g., theme name label updates, ANSI sequences absent when no-color).
- No panics and no hangs.

Acceptance:
- Scenario runs deterministically in CI.
- On failure, the artifact bundle clearly shows the last page and the toggle/theme state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.112857002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150108808Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.145700883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.141257332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150092478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.137009755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.131740545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Scenario runs deterministically in CI.
- On failure, the artifact bundle clearly shows the last page and the toggle/theme state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PurpleCastle","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.112857002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:12:39.341588186Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.145700883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.141257332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150092478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.137009755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.131740545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3eru","title":"Unit tests: filtering engines (jobs/logs/docs)","description":"Add focused unit tests for filtering/search logic (without spinning up the full UI).

Why:
- Filtering and search are easy to subtly break (case sensitivity, tokenization, pagination interactions).
- We want fast feedback during refactors.

Coverage:
- Jobs filtering/sorting:
  - query matches by name/id/service
  - status filters compose correctly
  - sorting is stable and deterministic
- Logs filtering:
  - level/service selectors work
  - free-text query matches expected fields
- Docs search:
  - match finding and next/prev navigation over a rendered buffer

Edge cases to include:
- empty query
- Unicode input
- very long query (no panic, bounded behavior)
- paste-like input containing newlines/whitespace

Nice-to-have:
- Add a small set of property tests (e.g., filter is idempotent, sorting preserves multiset) if it stays lightweight.

Acceptance:
- Core filter logic is correct and stays correct during refactors.
- Tests are deterministic and run fast.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.169792772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:27.066580206Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-1fxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:26.759858058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-212j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:25.449597433Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:28.296789159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.182594415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gt6","title":"Quality: README section for demo_showcase","description":"Document how to run (and trust) the showcase.

Include:
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` basics
- flags: theme/seed/no-animations/no-color/no-mouse/no-alt-screen, ssh mode, self-check
- keybindings summary + discoverability notes (help overlay, command palette if present)
- E2E instructions:
  - how to run the smoke tour and where artifacts/logs are written
  - how to run a single scenario for debugging

Also include a *feature coverage matrix* (self-contained):
- For each crate feature we claim (bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/wish/charmed_log/harmonica), list:
  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:
- Someone can run the demo and understand it without reading code.
- Someone can validate "everything works" without rediscovering the plan.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.297365816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:00.527452079Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:31.956437980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1knu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:34.482189415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.313191791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gbps","title":"Unit tests: Config parsing + env/CLI precedence","description":"Add focused unit tests for demo_showcase runtime configuration.

Why:
- The demo will have many toggles; config bugs create confusing UX and flaky tests.
- We want deterministic, documented precedence rules.

Test coverage (minimum):
- Defaults: Config created with no args/env is sane.
- CLI parsing: each flag maps correctly into Config.
- Precedence rules:
  - CLI overrides env where sensible.
  - `NO_COLOR` forces no-color unless explicitly overridden (if we support `--force-color`).
  - `--theme-file` must override `--theme` preset.
- Validation: invalid values (bad seed, bad theme name) produce clean errors.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not require a TTY.
- Failures include actionable assertions/messages.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.286603748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.307928300Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.307911148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.302950583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gp8c","title":"Fix Wish keyboard-interactive auth handling and session manager safety","description":"Fix Wish auth keyboard-interactive flow (russh handler missing), ensure session cleanup task is single-shot, and prevent channel count underflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:37.934158444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125584226Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125513313Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gyzr","title":"Code Quality & Reliability Hardening: Deep Review Findings","description":"# Epic: Code Quality & Reliability Hardening

## Origin
This epic captures all deficiencies identified during a comprehensive deep-dive code review
of the entire charmed_rust codebase conducted on 2026-01-27. The review examined all core
crates (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles, glamour, huh, charmed_log, harmonica) for:

- Bugs and correctness issues
- Thread safety and concurrency problems
- Performance inefficiencies
- API design issues
- Code duplication and maintainability

## Scope
The findings span multiple crates with varying severity:
- **CRITICAL**: Issues that can cause panics, data loss, or security problems
- **HIGH**: Issues affecting correctness or significant performance
- **MEDIUM**: Code quality, maintainability, and minor performance issues

## Goals
1. Eliminate all sources of unexpected panics (lock poisoning, bounds checks)
2. Ensure reliable I/O with proper error handling
3. Consolidate duplicated implementations for maintainability
4. Optimize hot paths identified during review
5. Fix all identified bugs and edge cases

## Dependencies
This epic's tasks should be completed before major feature work to ensure a stable foundation.

## Quality Bar
Each fix must:
- Include regression tests where applicable
- Pass all existing tests
- Pass clippy with -D warnings
- Be reviewed for thread safety implications","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.042212490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.062139404Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","hardening","review-findings"]}
-{"id":"bd-i0i9","title":"Docs: docs navigation (list of pages + preview)","description":"Implement a docs index + navigation experience.

Design:
- Split view:
  - Left pane: doc page list (filterable list)
  - Right pane: rendered content (glamour + viewport)

Behavior:
- Switching docs is instant.
- Preserve per-doc scroll position when switching away and back (optional but high polish).
- The list should reflect the authored docs content (title/subtitle) rather than raw filenames.

Integration:
- Hooks into in-doc search (search operates within the current doc).

Acceptance:
- Navigation feels fast and "real product" quality.
- Selection and (optionally) scroll state are preserved across page navigations.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.361710616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:02:15.397858253Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","list"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.387706158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:26.023997036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iwbw","title":"Dashboard: animated counters/progress (harmonica)","description":"Make the Dashboard feel alive with tasteful, physics-based motion (harmonica).

Why:
- Smooth motion is a signature Charm aesthetic.
- Animated counters and progress bars make metrics changes readable and satisfying.

What to animate:
- Counter values (requests/sec, latency, error rate):
  - spring toward target to avoid jitter
- Progress bars:
  - ease changes rather than snapping
- Optional:
  - a subtle transition when switching focus between dashboard cards

Constraints:
- Must not interfere with input responsiveness.
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - values snap directly to targets

Acceptance:
- Motion is smooth, stable, and never causes flicker.
- The dashboard remains readable even with rapid metric changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.093043228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:56:24.359362381Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:56:24.359295086Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-2myg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:19.483793101Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:20.705038373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.109088230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
